items.title: Sturm-trainer wil Feyenoord 'plezier in het voetballen ontnemen'
items.summary: Fabio Ingolitsch kijkt uit naar zijn officiële debuut als trainer van Sturm Graz in het Europa League-duel met Feyenoord. De 33-jarige trainer beseft dat hij met zijn ploeg de underdog is, maar wil de Rotterdammers het voetballen onmogelijk maken.

items.title: Stand Europa League: Feyenoord, FC Utrecht en Go Ahead staan er matig voor
items.summary: Speelronde 7 in de Europa League staat voor de deur en dus is het tijd om de balans op te maken. De drie Nederlandse deelnemers staan er niet al te florissant voor. Virtueel staan Go Ahead Eagles, Feyenoord en FC Utrecht allemaal op een plek die een Europese exit betekent.

items.title: Dit is de stand in de Champions League na zeven speelronden
items.summary: Er zijn inmiddels zeven speelronden achter de rug in de competitiefase van de Champions League 2025/26. PSV en Ajax staan er wisselend voor. Eén ploeg is nog altijd foutloos: Arsenal.

items.title: Hoe Zinchenko twee problemen bij Ajax kan oplossen
items.summary: Ajax heeft zijn gewenste versterking op de back-positie bijna binnen. Met Oleksandr Zinchenko (29) komt er opnieuw een voormalige Arsenal-speler naar Amsterdam. Daarmee hoopt Ajax twee zwakke plekken in de selectie aan te pakken.

items.title: 'Logisch dat PSV voelt dat het nog door kan gaan in de CL'
items.summary: Vanuit Engeland blikt clubwatcher Tim Reedijk uitgebreid terug op de nederlaag van PSV bij Newcastle United. Hebben de Eindhovenaren nog kans om door te gaan in de Champions League? Het wordt besproken in VI ZSM.

items.title: Would Man Utd 2008 really 'batter' Arsenal 2026?
items.summary: After being challenged by Theo Walcott, Wayne Rooney said the Manchester United side of 2008 would "batter" the current Arsenal team.

items.title: Football transfer rumours: Real Madrid leading race to sign Adam Wharton?
items.summary: Today’s rumours are squeezing a lot into a short time Kostas Tsimikas may be enjoying the Roman sunshine on loan, but Nottingham Forest are still peering longingly across the Mediterranean at Liverpool’s spare left-back. The arrival of Milos Kerkez has left Tsimikas firmly behind Andy Robertson in the Anfield pecking order but Forest’s interest has never gone away. Roma would be willing to end his loan period early provided they can find someone else first – understandable given he has only four Serie A starts this season under Gian Piero Gasperini, whose wing-back system does not suit the 29-year-old. Sunderland are reportedly clutching Noah Sadiki with the kind of white-knuckled intensity usually reserved for a North Sea ferry in a gale. Manchester United are admirers of the 21-year-old DR Congo midfielder and have even toyed with the idea of a player-plus-cash deal involving Manuel Ugarte, but the Black Cats have no interest in a deal. Sadiki’s value has apparently doubled since his £15m arrival from Union Saint-Gilloise and Sunderland seem in no mood to cash in just yet. The player has started every Premier League match under Régis Le Bris bar the games he was away for Afcon. Continue reading...

items.title: L'pool top English club in Money League; Utd fall
items.summary: Real Madrid topped the latest Deloitte Football Money League rankings, generating a record €1.2 billion ($1.4 billion) in revenue, with the top 20 teams represented clearing €12.4 billion ($14.5 billion) when combined, also a record.

items.title: Szoboszlai: 'I did my homework' on trick free kick
items.summary: Dominik Szoboszlai said he did his "homework" paid off on his trick free kick that gave Liverpool the lead in a 3-0 win at Marseille in the Champions League on Wednesday.

items.title: Barça win, but injury for Pedri 'not good news'
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said it's "not good news" after losing Pedri to a hamstring injury in Wednesday's 4-2 Champions League win away at Slavia Prague.

items.title: Title-winning car? Mercedes reveal highly anticipated W17
items.summary: Mercedes have provided a first look at the W17, the 2026 challenger that many believe will enable the Silver Arrows to return to the front of the grid.

items.title: Scorecard: Sri Lanka vs England, first ODI, Colombo
Why are there so many goalless draws in the Premier League this season?
items.summary: Passes, shots and goals are all down on last season. It might keep tacticians happy but it’s not as much fun By Opta Analyst Gerard Piqué spoke to his former Spain teammate Iker Casillas on his podcast last February and the topic of goalless draws came up. You might expect a centre-back and goalkeeper to be excited about the art of defending but rather Piqué suggested that teams should be punished for participating in goalless draws. “It can’t be that you go to a football stadium, spend €100, €200 or €300, and the match ends 0-0,” said Piqué. “Something needs to change. One proposal to consider would be that if the match ends 0-0, the teams would score zero points. Then the match would open up in the 70th minute.” Continue reading...

items.title: Birmingham’s major move shows where fiscal power lies in women’s football
items.summary: Ambitious owners and financial growth have allowed WSL and WSL2 clubs to assert dominance in the transfer market “If anyone didn’t take our ambition seriously, I hope they really do after this window, because it shows what we’re pushing for.” Amy Merricks was answering a question about Birmingham City breaking the second-tier transfer record to sign Wilma Leidhammar from Norrköping, but the head coach’s words could easily sum up the English January transfer window as a whole, as teams in the Women’s Super League, and in WSL2, demonstrate where the financial power lies in the women’s game. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Ridiculous amount of games' - has Haaland played too much football?
items.summary: Erling Haaland made a blistering start to the season but that prolific run of form has ground to a halt with only one goal in his past eight games.

items.title: Bridget’s View looks to double up at Newcastle
items.summary: We have a busy day’s racing on Thursday with flat action from Newcastle and Southwell, live on Sky Sports Racing...

items.title: McLaren want to simplify Norris-Piastri racing rules in 2026
items.summary: McLaren boss Andrea Stella says the team will attempt to make managing competition between their drivers Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri "simpler" during the 2026 Formula 1 season.

items.title: Djokovic's 399th Grand Slam match win propels him into Australian Open third round
items.summary: Novak Djokovic's 399th Grand Slam match win over Italian qualifier Francesco ​Maestrelli propelled him into the Australian Open third round.

items.title: 'Unique' Rogers looks to prove worth for Villa & England
items.summary: Morgan Rogers says he "can be anything" - while the Aston Villa star is talking about his positional flexibility, rather than boasting about his quality, his confidence is well-placed.

items.title: How to reach the Champions League knockouts: Can Chelsea, Barça avoid the playoff round?
items.summary: We're less than a week away from knowing which 24 teams advance from the league phase. Here's what's at stake with only one matchweek to go.
